title: event_log
description: The append-only event log — the PRIMARY source of truth for a loop. On resume, the checkpoint and its derived counters (cost_units_spent, iteration, attempt, node_states) are reconciled by replaying this log. Each node advance writes a pre_effect entry, executes the side effect, then writes a post_effect entry with the outcome; a dangling pre_effect with no post_effect is an in-doubt transaction. Modelled as {schema_version, entries[]}; entries[].seq is strictly monotonic.

seq.description: Strictly monotonic sequence number across the whole log.

kind.description: pre_effect/post_effect bracket a side effect; note = annotation; mutation = a structured plan change (carries mutation_type + reason).

to_status.description: Intended node status after the transition (pre_effect).

intent.description: What the effect intends to do (pre_effect).

idempotency_key.description: A stable key that makes re-running this effect safe. Required in practice for any non-idempotent effect so an in-doubt entry can be safely replayed.

result_hash.description: Hash of the effect's result (post_effect), for deterministic replay checks.

mutation_type.description: For kind:mutation — the class of change. A plan mutation must be typed + reasoned + evidenced, never an untracked edit (rule R39).

reason.description: For kind:mutation — why the change was made.

evidence_refs.description: For kind:mutation — evidence entry_ids that justify the change.
